title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.articleTitle: The Impact of Biomimicry in Design
content.text: For centuries, engineers have looked to nature for inspiration, a practice now (0) formally known as biomimicry. This field involves (1) biological systems and applying their principles to solve human problems. One of the most famous examples is the development of Velcro, which was (2) by the way burrs from plants stick to animal fur. More recently, architects have studied the structure of bird bones to create lightweight yet (3) building materials. These natural designs have evolved over millions of years to be highly efficient, often (4) human inventions in terms of sustainability. By copying the way leaves (5) sunlight, scientists are developing more effective solar panels. The goal is not just to imitate the appearance of nature, but to (6) its underlying logic. This approach has the potential to (7) industries by reducing waste and energy consumption. As we face global environmental challenges, biomimicry offers a (8) toward a more harmonious relationship with our planet. It reminds us that the solutions to many of our most complex technical issues may already exist in the natural world, waiting to be discovered by those who take the time to observe the intricate beauty and functional efficiency of life.

example.explanation: “Formally known as” is a standard phrase used to introduce a technical or official name.

questions.explanation: “Examining” implies a detailed and scientific look at something.
“Inspired by” is the standard collocation for creative or scientific influence.
“Durable” is the correct technical term for materials that last a long time.
“Surpassing” means going beyond or being better than something else in quality.
“Harvesting sunlight” is a common scientific metaphor for energy collection.
“Grasp” means to understand a difficult concept or logic thoroughly.
“Revolutionize” is a strong verb for causing a complete and dramatic change.
“Pathway toward” is a common figurative expression for a route to a goal.
